Video.summary: Graveyard Carz returns to conclude the epic builds of Chris JacobÆs 1968 GTX after it suffered a devastating fire, and the first of its kind 1970 Plymouth Superbird equipped with a Hellcat Crate Hemi.
Video.title: SEMA Has Eyes On A Hellbird

Image.alt: SEMA Has Eyes On A Hellbird
SEMA Has Eyes On A Hellbird
SEMA Has Eyes On A Hellbird

Video.summary: New Season, New Carz! With SEMA in their rear-view mirror, the ghouls begin the final assembly of a 1969 Plymouth GTX in Seafoam Turquoise. And when help comes up short-handed, Mark enlists what he likes to call his ōEcho Bomber.ö
Video.title: 68 GTX vs Echo Bomber

Video.summary: ItÆs the 100th Episode! Join Mark and Royal and watch the never-before-aired Pilot featuring some of the first Mopar restorations the original ghouls tackled over ten-years ago.
Video.title: Pilot Takes Flight

Video.summary: ItÆs a hectic day at the Graveyard. Dave jumps from car to car, installing and assembling what he can, Will struggles to keep focused on one project at a time, and Mark makes progress on his A100 build with the hopes of revealing at SEMA 2018!
Video.title: Surveying The Will To Work

Video.summary: Professional wrestler, actor, and former professional football player known for his time in the WWE and WCW as Goldberg returns to the Graveyard! This time, he brings the ghouls two brand new projects - one of which includes installing a brand-new 392 Crate Hemi in his modern Dodge Truck.
Video.title: What's Next?

Video.summary: The Ghouls say goodbye to the 1970 Superbird equipped with a Hellcat Crate Hemi by taking it out for one last drive. Then itÆs all hands on deck to get the beautiful second-generation Plymouth GTX in Seafoam Turquoise finished in time for the owner to see it for the first time since his father drove it.
Video.title: Performance with a Touch of Luxury

Image.alt: Performance with a Touch of Luxury
Performance with a Touch of Luxury
Performance with a Touch of Luxury

Video.summary: With a Plymouth GTX finished, the ghoulz refocus their efforts on the 1969 Dodge Daytona. With the owner wanting a full restoration, Mark and the Ghouls begin the tedious disassembly. Because of the carÆs pristine condition, they take extra effort to document and preserve as much as they can.
Video.title: Dissecting a Daytona

Video.summary: The unsung heroes of the body shop begin the restoration on one of the most requested renovations ever: a 1971 Dodge Demon. With several panels needing replacement, Mark and George show the intricate process of grafting an entire front-clip from another vehicle to replace the rusted and damaged original.
Video.title: Exorcize a Demon

Video.summary: ItÆs crunch time at Graveyard Carz, Mark and his cousin Dougie fire up the engine for our Turbine Bronze Charger while Dave assembles its firewall in preparation to install the drivetrain.
Video.title: Frankenstein's Work

Video.summary: Dave is ready to wrap up the 1970 Challenger R/T SE but is waiting for parts. Meanwhile, MarkÆs A100 build continues, and after a long and tedious body-work and paint phase, Will is ready to deliver Dave the 1969 B5 Blue Plymouth GTX.
Video.title: Look to the West for B5 Blue

Image.alt: Look to the West for B5 Blue
Look to the West for B5 Blue
Look to the West for B5 Blue

Video.summary: The Ghoulz have finished the 1970 Challenger R/T SE. As they take it out for the first test drive in over 15 years, Mark and the team reflect on the trials and challenges throughout the restoration process.
Video.title: A Challenger Reflected

Video.summary: Mark, Dougie, and Allysa team up to kick things into high-gear around the shop. They begin the restoration of a Challenger T/A, Will paints a 1968 GTX in B7 Blue, Allysa learns how dashes are assembled, and they install the drivetrain for the ultra rare, 1 of 2, 1970 Hemi Convertible Coronet R/T.
Video.title: Mark, Dougie, Allysa, Oh My!

Video.summary: Mark pulls back the curtain on the next four MoparÆs in desperate need of resurrection while also showcasing the fans favorite moments from Seasons 8 and 9.
Video.title: Shaking the Magic 8-Ball

Video.summary: Mark and the Ghouls are back, and they are ready to get to work! With a call in from the owner of the super rare 1 of 2 ever made, 1970 Coronet Hemi Convertible, Mark has to work fast to reach a looming deadline. At the same time, Will primes the body of our 1969 Daytona, bringing it one step closer to being back on the road.
Video.title: Rapping Up the Dash

Video.summary: While Mark is on a road test of the just completed 1968 Charger, its owner surprises the shop by arriving in person! While the owner waits, Will completes the paint on a beautiful 1970 Challenger in B5 blue. Finally, with Mark returned the owner receives his restored Charger and cooks a meal for the Ghouls as thanks!
Video.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

Video.summary: ItÆs time to hit the casino! Mark has been invited to a massive car show at the Little Creek Casino but before he can go there are a few things that need to be finished first. Mark and Doug disassemble the Phoenix æCuda, a car that survived a massive garage fire. At the car show, Allysa and Will interact with fans and find their ōpick of the show.ö
Video.title: Upshift Creek

Video.summary: Mark has once again taken Allysa under his wing and is teaching her the finer points of installing a grill and headlights on our 1972 Duster, meanwhile George is in the body shop attaching a tricky quarter panel to a 1969 æCuda which Mark has to check off the listģmilitary style? Finally, the Duster gets itÆs drive train and is finally reunited with its engine.
Video.title: It's A Duster Buster

Video.summary: Who better to explain the history and discuss the changes, year to year, of the Dodge Charger, than the Master of Mopar himself, Mark Worman. In this special episode of Graveyard Carz, Mark will show you unique differences, surprising insights, and cool features on multiple years of the same car: the Dodge Charger.
Video.title: Carbituary: Charger

Video.summary: The focus has shifted, with parts having arrived at the shop itÆs time to install the Grill, Bumper, Dash and Interior of our 1968 Turbine Bronze Charger. Mark and the Ghouls work their magic on the Charger until a familiar face shows up. With Royal there, Mark shifts the crew back to the 1 of 2 1970 Coronet and finishes the interior. Then, itÆs time for a test drive!
Video.title: 1.5 Million Dollar Baby

Video.summary: With SEMA 2018 looming on the horizon, itÆs time to address the elephant in the room. MarkÆs 1964 A100 Hemi truck, the Little Dead Wagon, built as an homage to Bill MaverickÆs Little Red Wagon. Will is set to prime and paint, but has a disagreement with Mark over the color. When the engine for the A100 shows up itÆs time to unbox one of the most powerful engines Graveyard Carz has ever put in a car!
Video.title: Willie, Paint it Black?

Video.summary: When it comes to classic hot-rod components, nothing gets people going like a multi-carb setup... the Tri-power. Long admired for their stellar performance and macho attitude, the tri-power has become a hard thing to find. So why find one? Why not build your own? Stacey walks you through the process of building your own tri-power out of old derelict parts. After that, he pulls out the big Mopar guns and shows what it takes to put together a six-pack. Then, Stacey gets down and dirty and shows you how to build some simple, ultra strong axle dollies by using some old scrap metal like the kind laying around your garage. Who said gearheads arenÆt into recycling?
Video.title: Building A Tri-Power

Video.summary: The Banshee is back from paint and we're ready to answer the questionģ does it still look like a Æ95 Miata? Has Stacey shown you how to create a completely different monster? Well, all we can say is the proof is in the paint and you are not going to believe how complete and affordable this transformation really is! Then, Stacey digs into the age-old question: Is it better to restore a car original, or modify it? The answer is up to you as he features a really clean Æ68 Plymouth GTXģ with some not-so-subtle modifications.
Video.title: V8 Miata Banshee Paint & Body

Video.summary: Stacey pulls out the tools and starts working metal as he dives into the subject of what to do when your floors are goneģ I mean literally goneģnothing there! ItÆs not as big of a problem as you may think! Stacey rolls in the Rat Roaster and shows you how to create solid metal floors from nothing. This show is loaded with tools and tech that will help you build anything from a small patch panel to a complete floor. While heÆs at it, Stacey also covers seat selection and placement so you have a place to plant your seat when you get ready to roll down the street.
Video.title: The Rat Roaster - Building Floors

Video.summary: What do you get when you combine Armor All, Tony Stewart and Stacey David with an iconic vehicle like the Corvette? You get a really cool car called ōOlÆ Smokeö and some lucky viewer is going to own it! ThatÆs right, Stacey takes a brand-new æVette' and shows you how to create your own 180+ MPH supercar for a fraction of the cost, using some aftermarket parts. How is that possible?... turbos baby!... and on this show Stacey shows you how to slap a pair of æem on the Corvette to run the horsepower numbers to over 600! If you have ever wondered about turbo charging an if itÆs right for your vehicle, this is a show you definitely want to see.
Video.title: Ol' Smoke Corvette Twin Turbos

Video.summary: OlÆ Smoke is back as Stacey finishes up the transformation of a brand-new Æ08 Corvette, into a full-on super car capable of running with any Ferrari, Porsche, or Lamborghini. ItÆs a brake upgrade, along with wheels and tires and some custom airbrushing, to not only make the car even more unique, but to also get it ready for itÆs new owner to enjoy. Then, Stacey takes OlÆ Smoke out and puts it through the paces showing once again that you can not only have fun building something cool, but also having just as much fun driving it when you are done. No doubt about it, somebody is going to get a special car when Armor All gives OlÆ Smoke away.
Video.title: Ol' Smoke Corvette Brakes Wheels Tires

Image.alt: Ol' Smoke Corvette Brakes Wheels Tires
Ol' Smoke Corvette Brakes Wheels Tires
Ol' Smoke Corvette Brakes Wheels Tires

Video.summary: What is the nastiest project that everyone has to do eventually? ThatÆs rightģ replacing old, worn out, nasty carpeting. Stacey rolls the Banshee back in the shop and shows you how to bring a very tired interior back to life with some tips and tricks that will help in replacing those old, worn out threads with some new ones. While we are on the subject of interiors, Stacey also digs into one of the best kept secrets in auto restorationģ painting a dash and door panels. If you would like to make your dash and door panels look like new again in just a few hoursģ for very little moneyģ and not have the paint flake off in a few weeks in big chunks, then this is the show you have to see!
Video.title: V8 Miata Banshee Carpet & Exhaust

Video.summary: Stacey takes a look at one of the first aftermarket parts that became available to the public back in the day: the performance air filterģ and man does it have an interesting story! From the lowly beginnings of fragile paper elements... to the massive, top secret facilities of industry powers like K&N, the air filter has a heritage that was born and bred on the race track! Then, Stacey digs into one of the neatest tools youÆll ever see and one that every metal worker should ownģ the Power Hammer. If you are interested in shaping metal and building your own panels, then you need to see this in action.
Video.title: K&N Air Filter Story

Video.summary: Stacey borrows an æ07 Jeep JK from a family and jumpstarts their project with the sole purpose of taking it to the next level. However, the real challenge is to build something the whole family will like and still be able to use on the street or off-road. If youÆve ever sat and stared at your project and tried to figure out what the heck to do next or what is the best thing to spend your time and money onģ this show is the one to see!
Video.title: Jeep Wrangler JK Suspension Lift
